title: citrus trifoliata subf. monstrosa
intro: Citrus trifoliata subf. monstrosa, also known as the trifoliate orange, is a deciduous tree in the Rutaceae family, characterized by its unique trifoliate leaves and small, yellow or greenish-yellow flowers. The trifoliate orange has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of limonene and other volatile oils. The health benefits of the trifoliate orange include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and provide relief from coughs and colds, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the trifoliate orange include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make cough syrups, digestive tonics, and other medicinal products.
